raw_title: Book Review: ‘Darkology,’ by Rhae Lynn Barnes
description: A new book by Rhae Lynn Barnes examines how minstrelsy once occupied the center of the nation’s cultural life.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Almost Life,’ by Kiran Millwood Hargrave
description: In Kiran Millwood Hargrave’s novel “Almost Life,” a passionate love affair between two college women gives way to a lifetime of what-ifs.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Playmakers: The Jewish Entrepreneurs Who Created the Toy Industry in America,’ by Michael Kimmel
book_title: ‘Playmakers: The Jewish Entrepreneurs Who Created the Toy Industry in America,’ by Michael Kimmel
description: In “Playmakers,” Michael Kimmel traces, and celebrates, the immigrant roots of the American toy industry. (Batteries not included.)

raw_title: Book Review: ‘The Feather Wars,’ by James H. McCommons
book_title: ‘The Feather Wars,’ by James H. McCommons
description: In “The Feather Wars,” James H. McCommons pays tribute to the nation’s first conservationists.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Everybody’s Fly,’ by Fab 5 Freddy
description: As his new memoir demonstrates, he himself would achieve fame as a visual artist, filmmaker, TV host and formative tastemaker.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Paradiso 17,’ by Hannah Lillith Assadi
description: “Paradiso 17,” by Hannah Lillith Assadi, considers the toll of displacement through the tale of a Palestinian émigré.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘A Scandal in Königsberg,’ by Christopher Clark
book_title: ‘A Scandal in Königsberg,’ by Christopher Clark
description: A new book by the historian Christopher Clark chronicles a nearly 200-year-old scandal with echoes of the present day.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘We’ and ‘The People Can Fly,’ by Joshua Bennett
book_title: ‘We’ and ‘The People Can Fly,’ by Joshua Bennett
description: Joshua Bennett’s two new collections, “We” and “The People Can Fly,” take different paths to the same destination.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Chain of Ideas,’ by Ibram X. Kendi
book_title: ‘Chain of Ideas,’ by Ibram X. Kendi
description: In “Chain of Ideas,” Ibram X. Kendi argues that a modern form of xenophobia has come to dominate conservative movements across the world.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Salt Lakes,’ by Caroline Tracey
description: In a new book, Caroline Tracey explores the mysteries and beauty of salt lakes.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Sisters in Yellow,’ by Mieko Kawakami
description: Mieko Kawakami’s novel “Sisters in Yellow” follows a group of dreaming and scheming young women through society’s margins.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Stay Alive,’ by Ian Buruma
description: In “Stay Alive,” Ian Buruma paints a picture of the city dwellers who survived in Germany under the Nazis.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘The Secret History of French Cooking,’ by Luke Barr
book_title: ‘The Secret History of French Cooking,’ by Luke Barr
description: A new history by Luke Barr chronicles the innovations, excesses and chauvinism of the French chefs who spawned a revolution in cooking.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘The Natural Way of Things,’ by Charlotte Wood
book_title: ‘The Natural Way of Things,’ by Charlotte Wood
description: Charlotte Wood’s “The Natural Way of Things” conjures a not-so-implausible world in which girls and young women are thrown into prison for their sexual shames.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Books Good Enough for You: The Storied Life of Ursula Nordstrom, Editor of Extraordinary Children’s Books,’ by Nancy Hudgins
book_title: ‘Books Good Enough for You: The Storied Life of Ursula Nordstrom, Editor of Extraordinary Children’s Books,’ by Nancy Hudgins
description: From 1940 to 1973, Ursula Nordstrom transformed kids’ books into real art and big business. A new middle grade biography attempts to capture her magic.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Night Night Fawn,’ by Jordy Rosenberg
description: Jordy Rosenberg’s second novel, “Night Night Fawn,” approaches a closed-minded matriarch with compassion, even at her child’s expense.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘In the Days of My Youth I Was Told What It Means to Be a Man,’ by Tom Junod
book_title: ‘In the Days of My Youth I Was Told What It Means to Be a Man,’ by Tom Junod
description: In an affecting new memoir, Tom Junod, a prizewinning magazine writer, grapples with unsettling discoveries about his larger-than-life dad.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘The Golden Boy,’ by Patricia Finn
description: From his perch in Hawaii, the hero of Patricia Finn’s first novel, “The Golden Boy,” revisits his dark past in rural Ontario.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Kids, Wait Till You Hear This! A Memoir,’ by Liza Minnelli
book_title: ‘Kids, Wait Till You Hear This! A Memoir,’ by Liza Minnelli
description: “Kids, Wait Till You Hear This!” is a familiar reminder that growing up in showbiz can lead to awards and adulation, but also to heartache.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘The Complex,’ by Karan Mahajan
description: Karan Mahajan’s new novel, “The Complex,” tracks the fortunes of a political family in a rapidly changing India.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Down Time,’ by Andrew Martin
description: In Andrew Martin’s keenly observed new novel, a group of friends navigate a society reshaped by the pandemic.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Nonesuch,’ by Francis Spufford
description: “Nonesuch,” the new novel by Francis Spufford, conjures a plot laced with magic to change the course of history.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Whidbey,’ by T Kira Madden
description: In “Whidbey,” three women reckon with the aftermath of sexual assault.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Gunk,’ by Saba Sams
description: “Gunk,” a novel by Saba Sams, follows a woman through the trials and tenuous jobs of young adulthood.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Stories,’ by Helen Garner
description: A newly released collection of the Australian master’s short fiction shows her sympathy, her virtuosity and her ear.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Chosen Land,’ by Matthew Avery Sutton
description: In “Chosen Land,” Matthew Avery Sutton argues that, despite the intentions of certain founders, the First Amendment guaranteed that the United States would be a godly country.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Days of Love and Rage,’ by Anand Gopal
book_title: ‘Days of Love and Rage,’ by Anand Gopal
description: In “Days of Love and Rage,” Anand Gopal creates an indelible portrait of revolution and civil war in Syria.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Lake Effect,’ by Cynthia D’Aprix Sweeney
description: Cynthia D’Aprix Sweeney’s new novel, “Lake Effect,” is the latest in a specific contemporary subgenre: “Four Adult Siblings Reconvene to Rehash Their Privileged but Fraught Adolescence.”

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Reproductive Wrongs,’ by Sarah Ruden
description: In “Reproductive Wrongs,” the classicist Sarah Ruden traces efforts to exert political control over family planning back 2,000 years.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Plastic Inc.,’ by Beth Gardiner

book_title: ‘Every Time We Say Goodbye,’ by Ivana Sajko
